Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or chronic kidney disease. No evidence of urinary tract infection is present. Blood glucose and HbA1c are within normal limits, making diabetes unlikely. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying renal pathology.
